meta.title: MS-FAE-3: DirectLake Modeling, Fabric Pipelines, and Governed BI Delivery

domains.name: SQL Analytics Endpoint and Lakehouse SQL

questions.stem: Which Microsoft Fabric capability provides a single logical data lake across Fabric experiences?

options.explanation: OneLake is the tenant-wide logical data lake underlying Fabric storage experiences.

questions.stem: A team wants to expose data that remains in Amazon S3 without copying it into OneLake first. Which Fabric feature should it use?

options.explanation: OneLake shortcuts let Fabric reference data stored in external locations without full physical duplication in OneLake.

questions.stem: Which statement best describes the difference between a Fabric Lakehouse and a Fabric Warehouse?

options.text: A lakehouse is Spark-first over Delta tables, while a warehouse provides a SQL warehousing experience
options.explanation: Lakehouses are oriented to Spark and open table files, while warehouses provide a SQL-focused warehousing experience.

options.text: A warehouse can store only CSV files
A lakehouse cannot expose SQL access
A warehouse cannot use OneLake storage

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about OneLake are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: It is the unified storage foundation for Fabric data items.
options.explanation: OneLake is Fabric’s unified lake and supports shortcuts to external data sources.

options.text: Shortcuts can reference data in other storage systems.
options.explanation: OneLake is Fabric’s unified lake and supports shortcuts to external data sources.

options.text: It is only available to Power BI dashboards and nothing else.
It requires each workload to provision a separate physical lake per item.

questions.stem: Which item automatically includes a SQL analytics endpoint for querying lakehouse tables?

options.explanation: A Fabric Lakehouse includes a SQL analytics endpoint for read-oriented SQL access to lakehouse tables.

questions.stem: A solution needs notebook-based engineering with Delta tables plus downstream BI over the same governed data. Which Fabric design is most aligned?

options.text: Use a lakehouse in OneLake and build semantic models over curated tables
options.explanation: A lakehouse supports engineering on Delta tables and can feed governed BI models from the same platform foundation.

options.text: Keep source files only in local desktops
Use paginated reports as the storage layer

questions.stem: What is the primary use of a lakehouse SQL analytics endpoint?

options.text: Read-only T-SQL querying over lakehouse tables
options.explanation: The SQL analytics endpoint provides SQL querying over lakehouse data.

questions.stem: When is a Fabric Warehouse typically a stronger choice than a Lakehouse?

options.text: When the workload is primarily SQL-centric warehousing and dimensional modeling
options.explanation: Warehouses are optimized for SQL warehousing experiences.

options.text: When users only need notebook markdown cells
When there is no tabular data
When Power BI must be disabled

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about SQL access in Fabric are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Lakehouses expose a SQL analytics endpoint for querying tables.
options.explanation: Fabric supports SQL through both lakehouse endpoints and warehouses, but they serve different experiences.

options.text: Warehouses provide a SQL-first analytical experience.
options.explanation: Fabric supports SQL through both lakehouse endpoints and warehouses, but they serve different experiences.

options.text: Dataflows Gen2 are the primary engine for DAX measures.
Spark notebooks require no workspace to run.

questions.stem: A team wants to minimize duplicate semantic definitions and keep curated SQL-facing objects close to governed storage. What is the best direction?

options.text: Model over curated Fabric lakehouse or warehouse objects instead of rebuilding logic in many disconnected files
options.explanation: Keeping curated SQL-facing objects in Fabric reduces duplication and improves governance.

options.text: Use only manually exported CSV files

questions.stem: Which Fabric capability orchestrates activities such as copy operations, notebook runs, and data movement steps?

options.explanation: Fabric data pipelines orchestrate multi-step data movement and processing activities.

questions.stem: A copy activity occasionally fails due to transient network issues. Which pipeline configuration most directly helps?

options.explanation: Retries on the activity help recover from transient failures without manual reruns.

questions.stem: Which Fabric item primarily uses Power Query for data shaping and transformation?

options.explanation: Dataflows Gen2 use Power Query-based transformation patterns.

questions.stem: Which TWO tasks are common uses of Fabric deployment pipelines? (Select TWO)

options.text: Promote items across development, test, and production stages
options.explanation: Deployment pipelines support structured promotion of Fabric items across environments.

options.text: Manage controlled release of BI and data artifacts
options.explanation: Deployment pipelines support structured promotion of Fabric items across environments.

options.text: Replace semantic models with raw image files

questions.stem: A team wants reusable transformation logic with environment promotion and fewer manual production changes. Which combination is strongest?

options.text: Use Dataflows Gen2 or notebooks for transforms and deployment pipelines for promotion
options.explanation: Reusable transform assets plus deployment pipelines support repeatable delivery.

options.text: Rely only on ad hoc desktop edits
Store production logic in chat messages
Use report bookmarks for ETL scheduling

questions.stem: Which scenario best fits Dataflows Gen2?

options.text: Low-code ingestion and shaping of data with Power Query
options.explanation: Dataflows Gen2 are designed for low-code data ingestion and transformation.

questions.stem: A nightly process must wait for upstream ingestion to finish before refreshing downstream models. What is the best approach?

options.text: Orchestrate the dependency in a Fabric pipeline
options.explanation: Pipelines are the orchestration layer for dependency-aware refresh and processing steps.

options.text: Only notify users by email and hope timing works

questions.stem: Which TWO characteristics are desirable in production-grade Fabric data pipelines? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Observability and parameterization are core traits of reliable pipelines.
Observability and parameterization are core traits of reliable pipelines.

options.text: Manual recreation of every activity in each workspace

questions.stem: Which schema style is generally recommended for Power BI semantic models because it simplifies filtering and relationships?

options.explanation: A star schema is the common recommended design for analytical semantic models.

questions.stem: What is the main role of DAX in Fabric semantic models?

options.text: Define calculations such as measures, calculated columns, and tables
options.explanation: DAX is the calculation language used in tabular semantic models.

questions.stem: Why are dimension tables typically related one-to-many to fact tables in a star schema?

options.text: So filters can propagate from descriptive attributes to transactional facts efficiently
options.explanation: Star schemas use dimension-to-fact relationships to support intuitive slicing and efficient analytics.

options.text: Because facts should never contain numeric values
Because dimensions must always be larger than facts

questions.stem: Which TWO practices generally improve semantic model usability? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use clear business-friendly names and measures
options.explanation: Clear naming and sound dimensional design improve usability and correctness.

options.text: Model around well-defined dimensions and facts
options.explanation: Clear naming and sound dimensional design improve usability and correctness.

options.text: Hide all fields regardless of purpose
Create ambiguous duplicate relationships whenever possible

questions.stem: A semantic model is difficult for analysts because sales and returns are blended into one wide table with repeating attributes. What redesign is most appropriate?

options.text: Refactor toward a star schema with conformed dimensions and fact tables
options.explanation: A star schema usually improves maintainability, performance, and user comprehension.

options.text: Store all logic only in report text boxes
Move the table to a slide deck

questions.stem: What does a semantic model in Power BI or Fabric primarily provide?

options.text: A business-friendly analytical layer over underlying data
options.explanation: Semantic models present curated tables, relationships, and calculations for analytics.

questions.stem: Why might a model author create measures instead of many static pre-aggregated columns?

options.text: Measures compute reusable business logic at query time in a controlled model
options.explanation: Measures centralize business logic and support flexible aggregations across filter contexts.

options.text: Measures are required for OneLake shortcuts

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about DirectLake are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: It is designed to query large semantic models over OneLake data with minimal import latency.
options.explanation: DirectLake is intended to query OneLake-backed data efficiently without the same ingestion pattern as classic import.

options.text: It aims to reduce the need to copy data into a traditional import cache first.
options.explanation: DirectLake is intended to query OneLake-backed data efficiently without the same ingestion pattern as classic import.

options.text: It requires every report to run only against Excel files.
It disables semantic modeling features entirely.

questions.stem: Which feature restricts which rows a user can see in a semantic model based on identity or assigned role?

options.explanation: Row-level security filters model data for different user audiences.

questions.stem: Why might a Fabric team use endorsements such as promoted or certified?

options.text: To signal trusted, recommended data assets to consumers
options.explanation: Endorsements help users discover and trust the right artifacts.

questions.stem: Which TWO controls commonly support governed BI delivery in Fabric? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Access control and RLS are central governance mechanisms for BI assets.
Access control and RLS are central governance mechanisms for BI assets.

questions.stem: A company wants sensitive financial data to be visible only to finance analysts, while the same report shell is used company-wide. Which design is most appropriate?

options.text: Apply row-level security or separate governed model access for audiences
options.explanation: Governed model access and RLS let different audiences use the same analytical assets safely.

questions.stem: Which Fabric-adjacent experience is primarily used to build interactive BI reports and dashboards for end users?

options.explanation: Power BI is the reporting and dashboard experience consumed with Fabric data assets.

questions.stem: Why are field parameters useful in reports?

options.text: They let users switch displayed dimensions or measures dynamically in visuals
options.explanation: Field parameters improve report interactivity by letting users change what a visual displays.

questions.stem: Which TWO practices usually improve report consumption experiences? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use a governed semantic model with meaningful measures
options.explanation: Governed models and audience-focused design improve report clarity and trust.

options.text: Design visuals around clear business questions
options.explanation: Governed models and audience-focused design improve report clarity and trust.

options.text: Expose every technical field by default regardless of audience

questions.stem: A report is popular but users complain that each page repeats slightly different revenue logic. What is the best corrective action?

options.text: Centralize the revenue definition in the semantic model
options.explanation: Centralizing business logic in the semantic model improves consistency across reports.

options.text: Hardcode separate visual-level formulas on every page forever
Export to CSV for each consumer

questions.stem: Which Fabric capability helps admins monitor capacity utilization and performance trends?

options.explanation: The Capacity Metrics app helps monitor workload and capacity usage.

questions.stem: Why can a good star schema improve report performance?

options.text: It reduces ambiguity and often creates more efficient filter and aggregation paths
options.explanation: Well-structured dimensional models usually perform and scale better than messy wide-table designs.

options.text: It guarantees no refresh is ever needed
It forces all visuals to use the same color

questions.stem: A large semantic model should refresh less data after initial historical processing. Which feature is most relevant?

options.explanation: Incremental refresh reduces processing by refreshing only recent partitions after the initial load.

questions.stem: Which TWO actions commonly help analytical performance in Fabric BI solutions? (Select TWO)

options.text: Design efficient semantic models and relationships
options.explanation: Performance depends on sound modeling and ongoing monitoring of workload behavior.

options.text: Monitor capacity and workload behavior over time
options.explanation: Performance depends on sound modeling and ongoing monitoring of workload behavior.

questions.stem: A DirectLake model is attractive to a team because it reduces which common bottleneck?

options.text: The need to repeatedly import data into a separate in-memory cache before analysis
options.explanation: DirectLake aims to reduce import latency by reading more directly from OneLake-backed data.

options.text: The need for any semantic model at all

questions.stem: Which admin-facing Fabric tool helps identify workload pressure on capacity over time?

options.explanation: The Capacity Metrics app helps admins understand utilization and workload behavior over time.
